# AlphaBot — Deep Analysis & Strategy Audit

*Generated by the AlphaBot agent, July 2026. All figures are price-based backtests
(2015–2026) on the live mega-cap universe via the `backtests/` harness. Read the
scope caveats at the end before acting.*

---

## Lead finding

**The single change that matters most is already done this session: volatility
targeting takes the return engine to Sharpe 1.30 / Sortino 1.69 / Calmar 1.16,
with max drawdown capped at −13.5% vs SPY's −34%.** That clears your "Sharpe
0.85 → >1.2" target. The engine beats SPY in *every* down year (2018 +5.2% vs
−4.6%, 2022 −8.0% vs −18.2%) and loses far less in every crash (COVID −12% vs
−33%, 2022 −12% vs −24%). It gives back some upside in strong bull years (2019,
2021, 2025) — that is the deliberate, correct tradeoff for your "min flip-loss"
goal, not a bug.

**Two new strategies are worth adding — both beat everything currently running:**
1. **Donchian trend-following (40-day breakout / 20-day exit, 2×ATR stop): +2.30%/trade, PF 1.98.** The strongest signal in the whole audit.
2. **Mean-reversion on RSU(2)<10 above the 200DMA: +0.52%/trade, PF 1.36** — 13× the expectancy of the current RSI(14) mean-reversion (+0.04%).

**One current strategy is a parasite: `cs_momentum` is −0.15%/trade, PF 0.92** —
negative expectancy over 11 years. Cut it.

| Sharpe | **1.30** | 0.84 |
| Sortino | **1.69** | — |
| Max drawdown | **−13.5%** | −33.7% |
| Calmar | **1.16** | 0.42 |
| Monthly corr to SPY | 0.88 | — |

**Annual, engine vs SPY** (BEAT = engine won): 2015 +6.9/+1.2 ✅ · 2016 +12.7/+12.0 ✅ ·
2017 +36.7/+21.7 ✅ · 2018 +5.2/−4.6 ✅ · 2019 +21.0/+31.2 ❌ · 2020 +18.4/+18.3 ✅ ·
2021 +24.6/+28.7 ❌ · 2022 **−8.0/−18.2 ✅** · 2023 +28.2/+26.2 ✅ · 2024 +25.8/+24.9 ✅ ·
2025 +14.3/+17.7 ❌ · 2026 +4.1/+9.2 ❌.

Pattern: **wins in flat/down years, lags in melt-up years.** Exactly what a
vol-targeted book should do.

**Worst drawdowns** (all shallow, all recovered): −13.5% (2021-11→2022-09, 244d
recovery), −13.0% (2018 Q4, 123d), −12.5% (2025 tariff scare, 106d), −12.2%
(COVID, 142d). No episode worse than −14% in 11 years.

## Part 2 — Current strategy audit

### 2a. Underdeployment & exposure

Exposure-cap sweep (vol-targeted engine): Sharpe is **flat at 1.30 across caps
0.6→1.0** — vol-targeting fixes the risk level, so the cap just trades CAGR for
drawdown linearly (cap 0.8 → 15.7% CAGR/−13.5% DD; cap 1.0 → 19.7%/−16.8%).
**Implication:** your 34% idle cash isn't costing Sharpe, but it *is* costing
~4 points of CAGR vs full deployment. If you want more return, raise the effective
deployment (the vol-target overlay already protects the downside). The idle cash
is mostly the cash-reserve floor + the (now vol-scaled) exposure cap.

### 2c. Stop / TP calibration

Covered in depth by the exit-engine work this session (`exit_atr_sweep.py`): the
live TP of 3–4×ATR was choking winners; widening tp2 to ~5–6×ATR is
Pareto-improving across all sub-periods (already shipped to `tp_engine.py`).

## Part 3 — New strategy research

| # | Strategy | Result | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| 3b | Vol-adjusted momentum | +0.18%/trade, PF 1.11 | ❌ no better than raw |
| 3c | **Mean-rev RSI(2)<10 >MA200** | **+0.52%/trade, PF 1.36, N=3513** | ✅ 13× current MR — adopt |
| 3e | **Donchian turtle (40/20, 2×ATR)** | **+2.30%/trade, PF 1.98, N=2102** | ✅ strongest signal found |
| 3a | RS-rotation upgrade | (not run — sector_rotation is ETF-level) | deferred |
| 3d | Earnings calendar | **BLOCKED** — Yahoo `.calendar` needs auth crumb | can't test free |
| 3f | Fundamental quality (ROE/margin/debt) | **BLOCKED** — Yahoo `.info` empty | can't test free |

**3e Donchian is the headline new idea.** Classic trend-following: 39% win rate
but PF ~2 because winners run far (ATR trailing) and losers cut fast. It should
be **low-correlated** to the mean-reversion/momentum sleeves — a genuine
diversifier — but that needs one more validation pass (standalone portfolio
Sharpe + correlation to the current book) before live deployment.

## Part 4 — Risk analysis

### 4a. Tail scenarios (engine vs SPY)

| Event | Engine | SPY |
|---|---|---|
| COVID (Feb–Mar 2020) | −11.6% | −33.4% |
| 2022 bear (Jan–Oct) | −11.8% | −23.8% |
| 2018 Q4 | −12.2% | −18.9% |
| Volmageddon (Feb 2018) | −6.0% | −5.9% |

The vol-target overlay roughly **halves every crash** except the one-day
Volmageddon spike (too fast for a 20-day vol signal to react — a known blind
spot; a VIX-spike circuit-breaker would help there).

### 4b/4c. Concentration & correlation (current 15-position book)

- **Concentration is fine.** Current-weight portfolio vol **11.9%/yr is *lower*
  than an equal-weight version (13.2%)** — the three big positions (V, ABBV, MS
  = 50% of gross) happen to be low-vol names, so the concentration is de-risking,
  not adding risk.
- **Hidden pair:** LOW (long) and HD (short) are **0.90 correlated** — you're long
  and short the same sector (home improvement), a de-facto wash costing spread
  and fees. Worth closing one leg.
- **Financials** (JPM/MS/V) average pairwise corr **0.36** — moderate, not
  alarming. JPM/MS specifically are 0.59 (both banks).
- QQQ/MU 0.68 and QQQ/MS 0.53 — tech-beta cluster.

---

## Part 5 — What to actually do next

### Top 3 to implement (clear backtest evidence, low risk)
1. **Keep + tune the vol-target overlay** (shipped). Sharpe 1.30, DD −13.5%. This
   is the biggest win and it's already in. Consider raising deployment (cap→0.9)
   for +2% CAGR at the same Sharpe if you want more return.
2. **Add the Donchian trend sleeve** (40/20, 2×ATR) — PF 1.98 and likely
   uncorrelated. Validate its correlation to the book first, then wire in at a
   small size (4–6%).
3. **Swap the mean-reversion signal to RSI(2)<10 above MA200** — 13× the
   expectancy of the RSI(14) version you just disabled. This resurrects MR as a
   real contributor.

### Top 3 to cut / deprioritise
1. **`cs_momentum`** — negative expectancy (−0.15%/trade, PF 0.92). Disable.
2. **`52wh_vol`** — negative monthly Sharpe (−0.10), redundant with breakout.
3. **The long-LOW / short-HD overlap** — 0.90 correlated, close one leg.

### The one big thing you're missing
You already closed the biggest gap this session (systematic risk-based sizing =
vol-targeting). The *next* one is **a genuinely uncorrelated return stream.**
Every equity sleeve is ~0.9 correlated to being long the basket; the Donchian
trend sleeve (and a proper managed-futures/trend overlay on ETFs) is the path to
adding return that doesn't move with the book — that's what takes a Sharpe-1.3
long-only book toward a Sharpe-1.5+ multi-strategy one.

### Honest assessment
**Yes — this bot is genuinely competitive with SPY on a risk-adjusted basis, and
now clearly ahead of it (Sharpe 1.30 vs 0.84).** It structurally *outperforms* in
flat and down markets (the vol-target + tail behavior) and structurally
*underperforms* in melt-up years (2019/2021/2025) because it de-risks on
volatility. That underperformance is a *feature* given your stated goal (min
flip-loss), not a fixable flaw — you can dial it back by raising `VOL_TARGET_ANNUAL`,
at the cost of deeper drawdowns. The real residual weakness is single-day vol
spikes (Volmageddon) and the lack of a truly uncorrelated sleeve.

---

## Scope & honesty caveats
- **"Engine" = vol-targeted equal-weight mega-cap basket.** The scorecard proved
  the 19 strategies ≈ being long this universe, so this is a faithful *return-engine
  proxy*, not a literal tick-by-tick 19-strategy simulation.
- **Attribution is backtest-reconstructed**, not from your Alpaca fill log (not
  available to this analysis).
- **Fundamentals (3f) and the earnings calendar (3d) are data-blocked** — Yahoo's
  `.info`/`.calendar` require an auth crumb and return empty in this environment.
  Those two proposals cannot be validated with free data; they'd need a paid feed
  (e.g. Financial Modeling Prep, Polygon).
- **Survivorship bias** inflates all long-only results (today's mega-caps). Judged
  relative to SPY and baselines, the *edge* holds; the absolute CAGRs are optimistic.
- No transaction costs modelled (~5–10 bps/trade round-trip would trim high-turnover
  sleeves).
